architecture_name: He installs emotional-flatness as maturity and trains her to suppress needs by rewarding her for requiring nothing, so that when she eventually reaches for connection, she finds a wall she helped build.
demographics: F/late-20s South Asian-American, M/early-30s white, Bay Area tech-adjacent
core_behaviors: Frames his emotional unavailability as rationality and maturity
Praises her for being low-maintenance, training her to suppress needs to keep his approval
Deploys rationalist vocabulary to position his flatness as the superior epistemic standard
satellite_signals: Calm-as-superiority framing; ease-as-compliment role-casting; self-contained low-need self-advertisement; first cool non-response to her emotional bid; rationalist register used to pre-frame her feelings as noise
dismissible_red_flags: His steadiness and low-drama presentation are genuinely attractive after chaotic past relationships; his rationalist vocabulary reads as intellectual depth; his self-sufficiency reads as maturity rather than emotional unavailability
scenario:
I've been seeing Ethan for about six weeks and honestly it's been the most low-key, pleasant start to something I've had in a long time. My last relationship was a lot, constant texting, jealousy, the whole thing. Ethan is just not like that. He told me pretty early on, I don't really do drama, I'm not going to blow up your phone or get jealous, I just think adults should act like adults. And I believed him. He's been exactly that.

He works in AI safety, lives in a group house in Berkeley, very much in that whole rationalist scene. Which I actually find interesting. He thinks rigorously about things. He reads Astral Codex Ten, talks about calibration and priors like it's just normal conversation, and it kind of is for him. I like that he's serious.

He's said a few times that he likes how easy I am to be around. Last week he said, you're not exhausting, I mean that in the best way. And I did take it as a compliment. I'm not clingy, I don't need constant reassurance, so it fits. He also mentioned his ex, said she was always falling back on her emotions, that she couldn't really keep up with the way he thinks through things. I didn't say anything about that.

The part I keep coming back to is from last Thursday. I'd had a genuinely hard day, something with my family, nothing huge, and I mentioned it while we were walking. He listened, then said, I mean, it'll probably be fine. I wouldn't give it too much power. And then we just moved on to something else.

I wasn't expecting him to fix it. I think I just wanted him to stay in it with me for a minute. He wasn't cold about it, he was totally calm. I told myself he's just not a feelings person, which I knew going in.

Is it weird that his response is still sitting with me a little?
